message:
fix(widgets): extend NoStyling/Skeleton degradation to badge, json_view, toast + debug_overlay tracing order fix

Fourth wave of the "respect DegradationLevel" cleanup landed in
this batch (see d8a889d1 and 47874347).  Four more widgets had
either unconditional renders (bypassing Skeleton short-circuit)
or baked their configured Style values into cells without first
checking apply_styling().

crates/ftui-widgets/src/badge.rs

- Widget::render(): snapshot deg, short-circuit on
  !deg.render_content(), and bind
    let style = if deg.apply_styling() { self.style }
                else { Style::default() };
  before the pad_left / label / pad_right render calls.  All
  three call sites now pass the local `style` variable to
  render_spaces() and draw_text_span() instead of self.style
  directly, so NoStyling produces an uncolored badge with the
  same layout.
- New tests:
    render_no_styling_drops_configured_style — builds a Badge
      with a distinctive fg+bg style, renders under NoStyling
      into a 10×1 frame, and compares cell (1,0) (inside the
      label span) against an expected frame built with a bare
      Badge; they must be identical.
    render_skeleton_is_noop — renders a styled Badge under
      Skeleton into a 10×1 frame and asserts every cell in the
      area is untouched relative to a pristine expected frame.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/debug_overlay.rs

- set_enabled(): the earlier commit (b1c26976 /
  47874347) hoisted the clear() + set_hover(None) calls
  out of the conditional branch but accidentally left the
  #[cfg(feature = "tracing")] tracing::info! call inside the
  `if prev != enabled` block that only fires when `!enabled`.
  That meant every enabled→enabled and enabled→disabled
  transition emitted a trace event but disabled→enabled (the
  common case) did not.
- Restructure to run the clear/hover reset inside
  `if prev != enabled && !enabled`, and then run the trace
  emission under a separate `if prev != enabled` that fires on
  both edges.
- Move `use ftui_core::event::MouseEventKind` from the top-level
  import block into the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block, since
  it's now only used by the test helpers after the main render
  loop no longer special-cases Moved/Drag events.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/json_view.rs

- Widget::render(): add the standard
    if !deg.render_content() { return; }
  short-circuit after the existing deg snapshot.  The existing
  code already honored apply_styling() for token colors but
  would still walk and rewrite every line under Skeleton
  degradation.
- New tests:
    render_no_styling_drops_token_styles — renders
      `{"key": 1}` with bold+italic key/number styles under
      NoStyling, then asserts cell (2,1) (first `"` of the key)
      has default fg + CellAttrs::NONE, and cell (9,1) (the `1`
      literal) has default fg + CellAttrs::NONE.
    render_skeleton_is_noop — full-frame buffer compare under
      Skeleton, asserting every cell in the 40×10 render area
      matches an expected pristine frame.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/toast.rs

- Widget::render(): same short-circuit addition right after
  the existing deg snapshot; the existing styling path was
  already gated on apply_styling() but the Skeleton case
  needed the early return.

Together with the earlier commits in this series, every Widget
in this crate that binds colors/backgrounds into Cells now
correctly degrades to plain content under NoStyling and
short-circuits to no-op under Skeleton.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
